methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $57M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

data.narrative:
Zama is a Series B-stage AI & Software company founded by Rand Hindi and Pascal Paillier. The valuation analysis draws on two methods triangulated against a peer set of five same-sector, same-stage private companies (Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, Nominal, and Photoroom), alongside one public comparable (PLTR).

The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x multiplier to Zama's $57M in total raised, yielding an implied valuation of $108M. This approach uses historical step-up patterns from Series B to later rounds. The peer-median method anchors to the median valuation of the five comparable companies at $5.0B, reflecting the wide dispersion inherent in early-stage AI valuations—peers range from $500M to $32.0B. These two approaches produce substantially different indications, reflecting tension between Zama's capital raised to date and the elevated multiples currently commanded by AI & Software ventures in the current market.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $92M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $2.6B. The wide band reflects material uncertainty around Zama's competitive positioning and revenue trajectory relative to the peer set. Key sensitivities include the applicability of Safe Superintelligence and Physical Intelligence comparables—both trading at pronounced premiums—versus earlier-stage peers like Nominal and Photoroom. Revenue and go-to-market traction remain critical refiners for narrowing this range.
